ground_truth.title: Project Management Certificate
ground_truth.main_content: Advance your career and gain in-demand project management skills with the Professional Certificate in Project Management from UC San Diego Extended Studies. This program is designed for anyone in any industry who wants to strengthen leadership, planning, and communication skills while learning to manage projects effectively from start to finish. With flexible learning, expert instructors, and a curriculum aligned with industry standards, you will gain the knowledge and confidence to lead successful projects in any industry regardless of project complexity. Enrollment Now Open for Winter 2026 · Start Your Certificate Today. The Professional Certificate in Project Management is ideal for anyone ready to build or advance their career in the field. Whether you're new to project management or an experienced professional, this flexible, career-focused program provides the practical tools, confidence, and credentials needed to successfully lead projects across any industry. This program is designed for: Those new to the field with no prior project management experience who want to start their career; Career changers ready to enter the project management field; Mid-career professionals looking to move into or advance within project management; Individuals preparing for the PMP Certification; who need required education hours; Professionals in technology, healthcare, construction, finance, and other industries who manage projects; Managers, team leads, and business owners seeking to strengthen leadership and project execution skills. Enrollment Steps: 1. Apply for Free: Click Apply Now at the top of the page and complete the short form. 2. Pay the Certificate Fee: Once accepted, log in to MyExtension, go to My Applications, and select Pay Certificate Fee to confirm enrollment. 3. Begin with a Foundational Course (Prerequisite): Scroll to the Course List, click a course title, and select “Add to Cart” to reserve your spot. Students must start the program with a foundational (prerequisite) course and may take other courses concurrently as long as they are enrolled in the prerequisite. Core and elective courses may be completed in any order afterward. Certificate Course Requirements: Foundational Course (Choose One): Project Management Essentials or Project Management Boot; Core Courses (All Required): Project Planning and Scheduling – Project Cost Management – Leading and Managing Change – Work Team Concepts and Skills; Electives (Minimum 4 Units): Agile Power Practices – Interest-Based Negotiation – Project Risk Management – Transformational Leadership – Purchasing and Procurement Process. Students may enroll in individual courses without applying to the certificate program. There is a $0.00 application fee. For students pursuing the certificate, there will be a one-time $125.00 certificate fee. For detailed information, please contact our Business, Professional & Legal Programs department. ProjectManagement@ucsd.edu. Foundational Course - Select One: Project Management Essentials (BUSA-40064) Units: 3.00, $695.00; Project Management Boot Camp (BUSA-40950) Units: 3.50, $995.00. Required Courses: Completion of foundational course prior to participation in BUSA-40715 and BUSA-40970 is required. Work Team Concepts and Skills (BUSA-40109) Units: 3.00, $695.00; Project Planning and Scheduling (BUSA-40715) Units: 3.00, $695.00; Leading and Managing Change (BUSA-40978) Units: 3.00, $695.00; Project Cost Management (BUSA-40970) Units: 2.00, $635.00. Choose Any Elective Courses Below to Reach a Minimum of 4 Units: Agile Power Practices (BUSA-40860) Units: 3.00, $695.00; Interest-Based Negotiation (BUSA-40128) Units: 3.00, $695.00; Microsoft Project (BUSA-40927) Units: 2, TBD; Project Risk Management (BUSA-40971) Units: 2.00, $635.00; Transformational Leadership (BUSA-40803) Units: 3.00, $695.00; Purchasing and the Procurement Process (BUSA-40392) Units: 3.00, $695.00. Process Improvement Elective Options: Earn an additional credential in Lean Six Sigma and satisfy the elective requirement for the PM program. Lean Six Sigma Yellow Belt (BUSA-40991) Units: 1.00, TBD; Lean Six Sigma Green Belt (BUSA-41006) Units: 4.00, $2,695.00; Lean Six Sigma Black Belt (BUSA-40995) Units: 6.00, $3,695.00; Lean Six Sigma Green and Black Belt (BUSA-40996) Units: 9.00, $5,695.00. Free Workshops and Sessions: Lean Six Sigma Information Session (Black/Green Belt) (INFO-70032) Units: 0.00, TBD. Student Resources & Benefits: Veterans Education Benefits: The Project Management Certificate program is approved for Veterans' funding. Eligible students may apply VA Education Benefits through UC San Diego Extended Studies. For assistance with applying your benefits to this program, please visit the Veteran Benefits office or contact us at unex-veterans@ucsd.edu. Workforce Funding Assistance: UC San Diego Extended Studies is proud to be an approved training provider for the Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act (WIOA). If you're a displaced or disadvantaged worker, you may qualify for federal assistance to help fund your education. To explore available funding and determine your eligibility, please reach out to one of the following: San Diego Workforce Partnership - workforce.org/career-centers; EDD - www.edd.ca.gov. Once you're approved, we're here to help finalize your paperwork, email unex-workforce@ucsd.edu, and we’ll guide you through the next steps. International Programs: International students are welcome to enhance their academic experience by enrolling in Project Management courses at UC San Diego Extended Studies. For application details and eligibility requirements, please visit the International Programs website or contact ipinfo@ucsd.edu. For questions related to visa requirements or program options, the International Programs team is available to provide guidance and support. UC San Diego Staff: UC San Diego employees, faculty, and their spouses or domestic partners are eligible for a 10% discount on most UC San Diego Extended Studies courses, including professional development, career training, and personal enrichment programs. For full details on this discount, visit the UC San Diego Blink – Extended Studies Discount page. Employees may also access additional educational benefits through the university’s Educational Benefits (EdBen) system, which supports funding learning opportunities. When submitting a request, select “Extended Studies Course” and indicate whether department funds will be used. For general information on education benefits and eligibility, visit the UC San Diego Blink Education Benefits page or contact Staff Learning and Development. Course Waivers and Transfers: Students may request to transfer coursework completed outside of UC San Diego Extended Studies, substitute a course taken within Extended Studies, waive a prerequisite course based on professional experience, or request an extension to the 21-quarter time limit for completing the certificate. To submit a request or access the appropriate forms, or if you’d like to review the steps and confirm whether a course can be transferred or waived based on your experience, please contact Tara Amin, Program Manager, at projectmanagement@ucsd.edu. Visit The Virtual Career Hub Today To Unlock Your Benefits! It's your one-stop shop for professional development and completely free. Explore Careers. Related Documents: PMP Handbook from PMI; Program Collaboration. FAQs: Can this program be completed entirely online? Yes! Students can take this program fully online in the convenience of your home or office. Some select courses may occassionally be offered in person. Will this program prepare me for a certification exam? Curriculum is based on the Project Management Body of Knowledge (PMBOK) developed by the Project Management Institute (PMI). Our program provides an overview of the PMBOK and teaches students how to best apply the various tools and techniques, in an application oriented setting. Although our courses can certainly help students to prepare for PMI’s CAPM (Certified Associate Project Management) or PMP (Project Management Professional) certification exams, the classes are not meant to be a test preparation. By taking courses you will receive the educational hours needed to be eligible to sit for PMI’s certification exams. For information about the PMP Certification, only proctored through PMI, please visit their website: http://www.pmi.org/certification/project-management-professional-pmp.aspx. Do the classes provide Professional Development Units (PDU’s)? Yes, our Project Management courses may be used to earn the Project Management Institute’s Professional Development Units (PDUs). One hour of classroom instruction equals one contact hour. Most of the three unit courses provide 27 instructional hours and therefore equate to 27 PDU’s. To claim PDU’s, simply print out your UC San Diego Division of Extended Studies transcript or course history document, accessible through My Extension, and provide to PMI. If I work full-time, will I still be able to complete this program? Yes. Our programs are designed for the working professional. Classes are offered in evenings from 6:00pm-9:00pm, online, or on Saturdays to help you find a class format that meet your schedule. Most students who work full-time take one or two courses per quarter. For those who are not employed or work part-time a typical schedule includes three or four courses per quarter. What will I earn at the completion of the program? Upon successful completion of the Project Management program, you will receive a Professional Certificate in Project Management from UC San Diego Division of Extended Studies. A Professional Certificate is defined as: Representing mastery of an established body of knowledge as is necessary to succeed in a field of professional practice; Material presented by multiple instructors, representing multiple viewpoints and a breadth of issues and practices; Curriculum reflecting timely changes in the field of study; complementing prior or simultaneous degree study in the field; Meeting a minimum of 120 classroom instruction hours (the Project Management certificate provides 180 classroom instruction hours); An award of a gold seal “full-size” certificate document upon successful completion. UC San Diego Division of Extended Studies—like all other UC San Diego schools, colleges and departments—is accredited by WASC through the University. All courses and certificate programs offered by UC San Diego Division of Extended Studies have been developed and administered in accordance with Extension policy and the regulations of the Academic Senate of the University of California. How do I get my certificate once I have completed the requirements? When you have paid your certificate fee and successfully completed all program requirements, you will need to request an audit by Student Services. To request an audit: In your MyExtension account, click on “My Courses.”; On the right side of the page under “My Certificates,” click on the name of the certificate.; On the right side of the page under “Tools,” click on “Request Certificate Audit.”; Complete and submit the form that appears. Once you have formally submitted your audit request, your certificate will be mailed to the address we have on file for you within ten business days. Grading Options – Letter Grade or Pass/No Pass: When registering, students may choose between a Letter Grade or Pass/No Pass option. As long as a student receives a passing score (70% or higher), they will earn the same certificate of completion and any related documents, regardless of the grading method selected. If you're pursuing employer reimbursement, we recommend checking with your company to confirm whether a Letter Grade is required for reimbursement eligibility. Students can update their grading option via their MyExtension student portal anytime before 12:00 a.m. on the final class meeting. When is the refund deadline for courses? The refund deadline for courses in this program is typically 3 days after the start of the course. This allows you to enroll in a course and participate in the first class meeting to determine if the course is a good fit. Then, if needed, either you may either transfer your enrollment to a different course or submit a drop request by emailing Student Services at unex-reg@ucsd.edu. The exact refund deadline for each section is listed in the section notes on the course page in our website, and you can also find it in MyExtension by clicking on “My Courses” and then the name of the course. Advisory Board: Sanjiv Erat, PhD, Associate Professor, UC San Diego, Rady School of Management; Jarazet Altamirano, Agile Leadership and Business Instructor, UC San Diego Extended Studies; Alexander Hempton, LSSBB, Performance & Analytics Department (PandA), City of San Diego; Paul Morris, MPA, PMP, Director Project Management Office, San Diego Housing Commission; Angela North, PMP, LSSGB, MBA, Project Manager, Cordoba Corporation; Sorayda Santos, Ed.D, PMP, VP, Project Manager, Banc of California. Related Programs: Accounting: Gain a solid foundation in accounting skills and an understanding of the environment in which accountants work.; Business Management: Build leadership, strategy, and business skills in AI, marketing, finance, and more with our Business Management Certificate.; Community Leadership: Develop leadership and public management skills to improve local government and community organizations effectively.
